title: Deaths from road injuries
subtitle: Annual number of deaths from road injuries. Road injuries include deaths of drivers, passengers, pedestrians and cyclists.
caption:
Deaths from road injuries

Annual number of deaths from road injuries. Road injuries include deaths of drivers, passengers, pedestrians and cyclists.

The choropleth map displays the annual number of deaths from road injuries across the globe for the year 2023, utilizing a color scale that ranges from light beige for lower counts to deep red for the highest figures. The legend indicates specific thresholds at 0, 1,000, 3,000, 10,000, 30,000, and 100,000 deaths. China and India are shaded in the darkest red, identifying them as the only nations exceeding the 100,000 death mark. A second tier of countries, including the United States, Brazil, Russia, Indonesia, and Mexico, appears in a medium-dark red, placing their annual death tolls within the 30,000 to 100,000 range. Several nations in Africa, such as Nigeria and the Democratic Republic of the Congo, along with Pakistan and Thailand, are colored in a lighter red-orange, corresponding to values between 10,000 and 30,000. Most of Europe, Canada, and Australia are depicted in pale beige or light orange tones, indicating figures generally below 3,000 deaths. Greenland and a few scattered territories are marked with diagonal hatching to signify that no data is available for those regions. The visual distribution highlights a concentration of the highest mortality figures in Asia and the Americas, while the lowest recorded numbers are prevalent across Oceania and Northern Europe.
